_description: Self-Service SaaS. Single end-user pool where anyone can sign up. All users get the same default permissions through org membership. Best for: consumer apps, developer tools, free-tier products, internal tools with open access.
_examples: Task manager — anyone signs up, gets read/write/delete on their own tasks
Developer portal — anyone signs up, gets API access
Internal dashboard — employees sign up, get read access

tiers.description: Service org — owns permissions and admin access
All end users. Self-registration enabled. Permissions inherited from service org.

service_accounts._note: Optional. Enable if your backend needs m2m API access (e.g., cron jobs, webhooks).

delegation._note: Typically not needed for self-service — all users have the same role.

sso._note: Optional. Enable if targeting enterprise customers who require SSO for their users.

teams._note: Typically not needed — single user pool with uniform permissions.

_org_diagram: Service Org (my-service)              [level 0 — admin, API key, permissions]
_zero_config_note: Users get permissions from org membership. No per-user grants. No callbacks. No cron. No maintenance.
